series_summary: Complete modern-era (post-GASB 34) financial dataset for City of Colorado Springs, 2002-2024 inclusive. All values primary-source verified against each fiscal year's own CAFR/ACFR Table 1 where available.

time_coverage.structural_floor_note: 2002 is the earliest year because that is when Colorado Springs first implemented GASB Statement 34 (consolidated accrual-basis reporting). Per 2005 CAFR Table 1 and 2009 CAFR Table 1 footnotes: 'The City began to report accrual information when it implemented GASB Statement 34 in fiscal year 2002.' Pre-2002 data exists only at the fund level and is not comparable to the post-GASB-34 series. Rate history (1996-present) is available separately in sales_tax_history.json.

files.description: Individual bond issues with maturity, rate, outstanding principal. Aggregate debt service by year through 2053.
Four pension plans + three OPEB plans, 10-year trajectory each.
27 overlapping jurisdictions with city's share of each.
10-year mill levy stack and assessed valuation history.
11 CSURA sales tax increment financing authorizations.
Utilities enterprise profile (ACFR-consolidated), 10-year revenue, 2024 operating detail, rate actions.
PLACEHOLDER — typed scaffold with nulls awaiting Colorado Springs Utilities standalone financial statements for service-line detail (electric/gas/water/wastewater/streetlight).
23-year primary government net position (2002–2024), GASB adoption events, structural changes, voter-approved tax events, correction log.
21-year city sales tax dollar series (2004–2024) + rate history back to 1996, with methodology divergences documented.

parcel_join_keys.description: How these datasets key to a parcel in the financial module
required_external_data: El Paso County Assessor parcel tax roll
El Paso County GIS parcel shapefile
CSURA project area shapefiles (from City Clerk)
computed_fields_per_parcel: share_of_overlapping_debt = sum over applicable jurisdictions of (parcel_AV / jurisdiction_AV * jurisdiction_debt)

correction_log_summary: 2010 primary gov net position: was $2,338,901,049, corrected to $3,137,967,812 (Net investment in capital assets row mis-identified as total)
2015 primary gov net position: was $2,977,767,902, corrected to $2,933,293,322 (same extraction error on a different year)

open_loops.item: Colorado Springs Utilities standalone financial statements

open_loops.status: image-based, OCR deferred — text body not material given 2009 CAFR covers the Great Recession narrative adequately
image-based, would only matter if intra-year restatements are of interest; overlap years (2006-2009) already verified via 2009 CAFR
